title: Financial administrative occupations n.e.c.
description: Analyze and coordinate the ongoing logistical functions of a firm or organization. Responsible for the entire life cycle of a product, including acquisition, distribution, internal allocation, delivery, and final disposal of resources. Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis, cost containment, capacity enhancement, routing and shipment optimization, or information management. Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes. May manage route activity including invoicing, electronic bills, and shipment tracing. (Based on 33 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Weigh envelopes containing statements to determine correct postage and affix postage, using stamps or metering equipment.

Examine and review checks to verify endorsements and other required information before processing payments.
Advise clients on their financial situations, including debt repayment strategies and goal setting.

Identify and resolve discrepancies in financial records, transactions, or customer accounts.

Order supplies and manage inventory relevant to financial operations or fundraising events.
Perform clerical tasks such as filing, mailing, and other administrative duties as needed.
Transport materials for processing or events while ensuring compliance with safety and operational standards.
Communicate effectively with clients and colleagues through various channels, including phone, email, and in-person.
Maintain a clean and orderly environment in the workspace, particularly in financial and operational areas.
Contact insurance companies to check on claims payments and write appeal letters for denied claims.
Inform individuals about available financial assistance programs and provide informed recommendations.
Assist in selecting financial award candidates using electronic databases for loan eligibility certification.
Maintain and review account records, updating them according to status changes.
Counsel clients on personal and family financial issues, including debt management.
Arrange for debt repayment or establish repayment schedules based on customers' financial situations.
Calculate the amount of debt and available funds to plan payoff methods and estimate liquidation time.
Trace delinquent customers to new addresses by inquiring with relevant organizations.
Match order forms with invoices and record necessary information to maintain accurate accounts.
Debit, credit, and total accounts using specialized accounting software for financial recordkeeping.
Reconcile records of bank transactions and financial statements to ensure accuracy.
Provide project management services, including analysis of technical data.
Manage logistical aspects of product life cycles, ensuring efficient coordination and minimizing obsolescence.
Develop proposals that include documentation for estimates and project requirements.
Perform system lifecycle cost analysis and develop component studies for logistics improvements.
Collaborate with departments to meet customer requirements and manage sales opportunities.
Monitor industry standards and trends to improve logistics planning and execution.
Analyze data to develop solutions or alternative methods for logistics processes.
Identify opportunities for inventory reductions and analyze logistics data involving customer service, forecasting, and transportation.

Evaluate the effectiveness of current or future logistical processes and recommend improvements to existing or planned logistics processes.
Provide logistics technology or information to support effective product, equipment, or service manufacturing or delivery.
Conduct logistics studies or analyses, including time studies, network analyses, or supply chain analyses.
Determine feasibility of designing new facilities or modifying existing facilities based on cost, space, and technical requirements.
Develop or maintain cost estimates, forecasts, or models for logistics uses, such as cost estimating or demand forecasting.
Prepare or validate documentation on automated logistics or information management systems, and maintain logistics records.
Track product flow from origin to delivery and monitor inventory transactions to assess shipping, receiving, and inventory integrity.
Communicate with logistics management teams to optimize service levels, maintain efficiency, or minimize costs.
Propose logistics solutions for customers and review contracts to determine logistics or support requirements.
Maintain databases of logistics information and prepare reports on logistics performance measures, such as delivery rates and inventory turns.
Manage systems to ensure that pricing structures reflect logistics costing and compute reporting metrics for analysis.
Evaluate the use of technologies to improve transportation efficiency and develop logistics metrics for performance evaluation.
Inspect outgoing work for compliance with specifications, notify departments when supplies are low, and check inventory for requested merchandise.
Read incoming correspondence to ascertain the nature of writers' concerns and determine disposition.
Compose letters in reply to correspondence concerning requests for merchandise, damage claims, credit inquiries, or billing issues.
Route correspondence to other departments for reply and maintain files to show correspondence activities.
Gather records pertinent to problems, review for completeness and attach them to correspondence as necessary.
Compile data from records to prepare periodic reports and process orders for goods requested in correspondence.
Prepare documents and correspondence, such as damage claims, credit inquiries, invoices, and service complaints.
Review correspondence for format and typographical accuracy, then submit for signature after assembling necessary copies.
Confer with company personnel regarding the feasibility of complying with writers' requests and present explanations of governing rules.
Ensure that money collected is properly recorded and secured, and compute costs of records furnished, writing letters for payment.
Type acknowledgment letters to persons sending correspondence and prepare records for shipment by certified mail.
